desc: Bergen is Norway’s rain-washed gateway to the fjords, framed by seven mountains and a historic harbor. Visit for colorful Bryggen lanes, seafood markets, scenic funicular viewpoints, and easy access to some of Scandinavia’s most dramatic coastal landscapes.
hls: Major departure point for fjord cruises
Compact center with walkable harbor core

todo.name: Wander Bryggen wooden wharf
todo.desc: Explore narrow passageways, artisan shops, and Hanseatic-era structures that define Bergen’s maritime identity.

todo.name: Ride the Fløibanen to Mount Fløyen
todo.desc: Take the funicular for city-and-fjord panoramas, then continue on forest trails if weather allows.

todo.name: Bergen Fish Market lunch
todo.desc: Sample local seafood such as fish soup, shrimp, and salmon near the harborfront stalls.

todo.name: Troldhaugen (Grieg Museum)
todo.desc: Visit composer Edvard Grieg’s home and concert hall for a quieter cultural stop outside the core center.

todo.name: Day cruise to Mostraumen or Hardanger
todo.desc: Use Bergen as a launch point for narrow fjords, waterfalls, and steep mountain walls.

todo.name: Ulriken cable car and ridge walk
todo.desc: Go up Mount Ulriken for higher alpine-style views and, in good weather, traverse back toward Fløyen.

wifi.note: Excellent in city hotels and cafés; reliable enough for video calls even on rainy days.

prac.fltNote: Seasonal nonstop Gatwick flights may run; otherwise connect via Oslo, Amsterdam, or Copenhagen.
prac.visa: Norway is in Schengen; many travelers (including US/UK) can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free.
alerts: Frequent rain and wind can disrupt fjord cruises and mountain cable car operations.

sweet: May to August has the longest days and best hiking windows, though showers remain possible year-round.
